hazards.hazardous_event: Tool reports no violations on a file that contains real safety violations (false-negative), leading developer to believe code is safe when it is not
hazards.safe_state: Developer independently reviews code and uses additional static analysis tools
hazards.safety_goal: cfusa shall not suppress or omit any finding for which the rule engine is configured to detect

hazards.hazardous_event: Tool reports excessive false-positives causing developer to disable checks or ignore findings, masking real violations
hazards.safe_state: Developer uses disposition system to explicitly accept findings with rationale
hazards.safety_goal: cfusa shall limit false-positive rate to a level that does not cause systematic check-disabling

hazards.hazardous_event: Command injection via unsanitised --from/--to git refs passed to popen(), allowing arbitrary shell execution on developer machine
hazards.safe_state: validate_git_ref() whitelist prevents injection; tool exits 1 with error message
hazards.safety_goal: cfusa impact shall validate all git ref arguments before constructing shell commands

hazards.hazardous_event: SBOM or qualification record is generated with incorrect checksums or version info, leading to incorrect evidence being submitted to a safety case
hazards.safe_state: Reviewer independently verifies SBOM checksums against source
hazards.safety_goal: cfusa release shall compute SHA-256 checksums deterministically and embed correct project/version from .cfusa.json

hazards.hazardous_event: Incorrect ASIL determination output (wrong table lookup) causes developer to under-design safety measures for a hazardous item
hazards.safe_state: Developer cross-checks ASIL with ISO 26262-3:2018 Table 4 directly
hazards.safety_goal: cfusa hara asil shall implement ISO 26262-3:2018 Table 4 correctly for all S/E/C combinations
